title: conospermum longifolium subsp. angustifolium
intro: Conospermum longifolium subsp. angustifolium, also known as the narrow-leaved conospermum, is a subspecies of plant in the family Proteaceae, native to Western Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Conospermum longifolium subsp. angustifolium have been traditionally used to reduce fever, ease respiratory issues, and support digestive health, due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often made into teas or infusions, which can be consumed to reap the plant's health benefits, including its potential to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
